usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'la'. Very common in cooking. Plural: carottes
common_collocations: carottes râpées, soupe aux carottes, jus de carotte
image_context: Fresh orange carrots with green tops on a kitchen counter
example_1: Je coupe les carottes pour le dîner.
example_1_translation: I cut the carrots for dinner.
example_2: Le lapin mange des carottes avec plaisir.
example_2_translation: The rabbit eats carrots with pleasure.
example_3: Les carottes sont riches en vitamines.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced kah-ROH-tuh. Stress on second syllable. From Italian 'carota'.
memory_hook: Carotte = carrot. Orange vegetable associated with health.

secondary_meaning: a red fruit used as a vegetable in cooking
usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'la'. Technically a fruit, culinarily a vegetable. Plural: tomates
common_collocations: sauce tomate, salade de tomates, purée de tomate
image_context: Red ripe tomatoes on a vine in a garden
example_1: Je mets des tomates dans ma salade.
example_1_translation: I put tomatoes in my salad.
example_2: Les tomates sont rouges et mûres.
example_2_translation: The tomatoes are red and ripe.
example_3: Elle fait une sauce tomate délicieuse.
example_3_translation: She makes a delicious tomato sauce.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced toh-MAHT. Stress on second syllable. From Spanish 'tomatl'.
memory_hook: Tomate = tomato. Red fruit used as vegetable.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'la tomate' (feminine). Botanically a fruit, not a vegetable.

secondary_meaning: a sweet baked dessert made from flour, eggs, and sugar
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Plural is irregular: 'gâteaux' (not 'gateaus')
common_collocations: gâteau au chocolat, gâteau d'anniversaire, morceau de gâteau
image_context: A decorated birthday cake with candles and colorful frosting
example_1: Ma mère fait un gâteau pour mon anniversaire.
example_1_translation: My mother makes a cake for my birthday.
example_2: Ce gâteau au chocolat est délicieux.
example_3: Nous avons mangé deux parts de gâteau.
example_3_translation: We ate two slices of cake.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced gah-TOH. Stress on second syllable. Has circumflex accent: â.
memory_hook: Gâteau = cake. Sweet dessert for celebrations.

secondary_meaning: a colorless, odorless liquid essential for life
usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'l'' (before vowel). Usually uncountable. Plural: eaux
common_collocations: verre d'eau, eau pure, eau minérale, eau du robinet
image_context: A clear glass of water with ice cubes on a table
example_1: Je bois de l'eau tous les jours.
example_2: Un verre d'eau, s'il vous plaît.
example_3: L'eau est essentielle pour la vie.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced OH. Single syllable. Feminine despite vowel elision.
memory_hook: Eau = water. Most essential drink. Simple French word.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'l'eau' (feminine, despite sounding neutral). Elision: 'l'' not 'la'.

secondary_meaning: a liquid extracted from fruit or vegetables
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Usually uncountable. Plural: jus (same form or juices)
common_collocations: jus de fruit, jus frais, jus naturel, jus pressé
image_search_query: le jus verre orange petit déjeuner
image_context: A glass of fresh orange juice with slices of orange
example_1: Je bois du jus d'orange le matin.
example_1_translation: I drink orange juice in the morning.
example_2: Il y a du jus de pomme dans le frigo.
example_2_translation: There is apple juice in the fridge.
example_3: Ce jus de fruit est très savoureux.
example_3_translation: This fruit juice is very tasty.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced zhoo. One syllable. 'j' sounds like 'zh'.
memory_hook: Jus = juice. Extracted liquid from fruit. From Latin 'iussum'.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'le jus' (masculine). Also means 'gravy' in cooking contexts.

secondary_meaning: a beverage made by steeping dried tea leaves in hot water
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Usually uncountable. Plural: thés (types of tea)
common_collocations: tasse de thé, thé chaud, thé froid, thé vert
image_context: A steaming cup of tea on a saucer with a tea bag
example_1: Je prends un thé chaud en hiver.
example_1_translation: I have a hot tea in winter.
example_2: Le thé est prêt, tu veux une tasse?
example_2_translation: The tea is ready, do you want a cup?
example_3: Elle boit du thé vert chaque matin.
example_3_translation: She drinks green tea every morning.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced teh. One syllable. 'th' sounds like 't'.
memory_hook: Thé = tea. Hot beverage from leaves. From Chinese 'chai'.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'le thé' (masculine). Accent: 'é' at the end.

usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'la'. Special character: 'œ' ligature. Plural: sœurs
common_collocations: grande sœur, petite sœur, ma sœur
image_context: Two sisters smiling together while taking a selfie
example_1: Ma sœur a les cheveux longs.
example_2: Je joue avec ma sœur le week-end.
example_2_translation: I play with my sister on the weekend.
example_3: Mes sœurs et moi allons au cinéma.
example_3_translation: My sisters and I go to the cinema.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced SER. One syllable. 'œ' is ligature (oe sound).
memory_hook: Sœur = sister. Female family member. Special 'œ' character.

usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Compound noun: 'grand' + 'père'. Plural: grands-pères
common_collocations: mon grand-père, chez grand-père, grand-père maternel
image_context: An elderly grandfather playing chess with his grandson
example_1: Mon grand-père raconte de bonnes histoires.
example_2: Je visite mon grand-père le dimanche.
example_2_translation: I visit my grandfather on Sundays.
example_3: Le grand-père aime jouer aux échecs.
example_3_translation: The grandfather likes to play chess.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced grahn-PEH-ruh. Stress on second syllable.
memory_hook: Grand-père = grand (big) + père (father). Big father = grandfather.

usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'la'. Compound noun: 'grand' + 'mère'. Plural: grand-mères
common_collocations: ma grand-mère, chez grand-mère, grand-mère paternelle
image_context: A kind grandmother baking cookies in the kitchen with her granddaughter
example_1: Ma grand-mère fait de bonnes tartes.
example_2: Je vais chez ma grand-mère après l'école.
example_2_translation: I go to my grandmother's house after school.
example_3: La grand-mère m'a donné un cadeau.
example_3_translation: The grandmother gave me a gift.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced grahn-MEHR. Stress on second syllable.
memory_hook: Grand-mère = grand (big) + mère (mother). Big mother = grandmother.

usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Common school item. Plural: stylos
common_collocations: stylo bleu, stylo noir, stylo bille
image_context: A blue ballpoint pen on an open notebook
example_1: J'ai besoin d'un stylo pour écrire.
example_1_translation: I need a pen to write.
example_2: Le stylo bleu est sur la table.
example_2_translation: The blue pen is on the table.
example_3: Prête-moi un stylo, s'il te plaît.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced STEE-loh. Stress on first syllable. From Latin 'stylus'.
memory_hook: Stylo = pen. Essential school supply. Simple French word.

secondary_meaning: a written work bound together with pages
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Very common. Plural: livres
common_collocations: lire un livre, bibliothèque de livres, livre d'école
image_context: An open book on a wooden table with reading glasses nearby
example_1_translation: I am reading an interesting book.
example_3: Elle emprunte des livres à la bibliothèque.
example_3_translation: She borrows books from the library.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced LEEV-ruh. Stress on first syllable. From Latin 'liber'.
memory_hook: Livre = book. A bound collection of written pages.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'le livre' (masculine). Not 'la livre' (pound in other contexts).

secondary_meaning: a container used for carrying items
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Can be any type of bag. Plural: sacs
common_collocations: sac à dos, sac d'école, sac lourd
image_context: A student's backpack on a chair near a school desk
example_1: Je mets mes livres dans mon sac.
example_1_translation: I put my books in my bag.
example_2: Le sac est lourd ce matin.
example_2_translation: The bag is heavy this morning.
example_3: Où est ton sac à dos?
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced sahk. One syllable. Similar to English 'sack'.
memory_hook: Sac = bag. Container for carrying items.

usage_notes: Proper noun. Uses feminine article 'la'. Not pluralized.
common_collocations: la France, en France, le drapeau français
image_context: The Eiffel Tower in Paris with the French flag
example_1: La France est un beau pays.
example_2: Je veux visiter la France un jour.
example_2_translation: I want to visit France one day.
example_3: La capitale de la France est Paris.
example_3_translation: The capital of France is Paris.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced FRAHNS. One syllable. Stress on first syllable.
memory_hook: France = a country in Europe. Capital: Paris. Known for culture and wine.

usage_notes: Proper noun. Uses feminine article 'l'' (before vowel). Not pluralized.
image_context: The Brandenburg Gate in Berlin, Germany
example_1_translation: Germany is a country in Europe.
example_3: La capitale de l'Allemagne est Berlin.
example_3_translation: The capital of Germany is Berlin.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced ahl-mah-YUH. Stress on second syllable.
memory_hook: Allemagne = Germany. Central European country. Capital: Berlin.
common_mistakes: Proper noun: capital 'A'. Gender: feminine. Elision: 'l'' not 'la'.

usage_notes: Proper noun. Uses feminine article 'l'' (before vowel). Not pluralized.
image_context: A beautiful Spanish beach with a Barcelona skyline in the background
example_2: Nous allons en Espagne pour les vacances.
example_2_translation: We are going to Spain for vacation.
example_3: La capitale de l'Espagne est Madrid.
example_3_translation: The capital of Spain is Madrid.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced eh-spahn-YUH. Stress on last syllable.
memory_hook: Espagne = Spain. Southern European country. Capital: Madrid.
common_mistakes: Proper noun: capital 'E'. Gender: feminine. Elision: 'l'' not 'la'.
